items.title: AUD/JPY Price Forecast: Softens below 111.00, but mildly bullish tone prevails
items.summary: The AUD/JPY cross extends the decline to near 110.85 during the early European session on Wednesday. The Australian Dollar (AUD) softens against the Japanese Yen (JPY) amid cooler-than-expected Australian inflation data and uncertainty surrounding US-Iran talks.

items.title: Iran says fired missiles at Israel and US forces in bases in Kuwait, Jordan and Bahrain
items.summary: Iran's Revolutionary Guards said on Wednesday that they had fired missiles at Israel as well as military bases hosting US forces in Kuwait, Jordan and Bahrain.

items.title: US Dollar Index holds above 99.00 as US-Iran talks stir uncertainty
items.summary: The US Dollar Index (DXY), which measures the value of the US Dollar (USD) against six major currencies, extends its gains for the second consecutive day, trading around 99.30 during the Asian hours on Wednesday.

items.title: RBNZ’s Conway sees economic slack shaping response to oil shock
items.summary: Reserve Bank of New Zealand Chief Economist Paul Conway said that the central bank sees lingering slack in the economy that will shape how aggressively it responds to the inflationary aftershocks of higher oil prices, Bloomberg reported on Wednesday.

items.title: Canadian Dollar struggles as Oil prices continue to ease
items.summary: USD/CAD extends gains for the second successive day, trading around 1.3770 during the Asian hours on Wednesday. The pair gains ground as crude oil prices soften on the easing geopolitical tone, weighing on the commodity-linked Canadian Dollar (CAD).

items.title: UK CPI set to show sticky inflation in February as markets price a BoE rate hike
items.summary: The UK Office for National Statistics (ONS) will release the February Consumer Price Index (CPI) figures on Wednesday at 07:00 GMT, a print that will matter for markets. Consensus expectations point to inflation pressures keeping their grasp on the economy.

items.title: WTI slumps below $87.00 as Trump signals Iran talks
items.summary: West Texas Intermediate (WTI), the US crude oil benchmark, is trading around $86.85 during the Asian trading hours on Wednesday. The WTI price falls as the US-Iran talks signal hope for Middle East peace.

items.title: Japanese Yen receives support following BoJ Meeting Minutes
items.summary: USD/JPY edges lower after registering gains in the previous day, trading around 158.70 during the Asian hours on Wednesday. The pair weakens as the Japanese Yen (JPY) gains support following the release of the Bank of Japan’s (BoJ) January Meeting Minutes.

items.title: PBOC sets USD/CNY reference rate at 6.8911 vs. 6.8943 previous
items.summary: On Wednesday, the People’s Bank of China (PBOC) sets the USD/CNY central rate for the trading session ahead at 6.8911 compared to the previous day's fix of 6.8943 and 6.8819 Reuters estimate.

items.title: Australian Dollar reacts little to soft CPI report; AUD/USD consolidates around 0.7000
items.summary: The AUD/USD pair continues with its struggle to gain any meaningful traction and extends sideways consolidative price moves during the Asian session on Wednesday.

items.title: BoJ Minutes: Members agree rate hikes pppropriate if outlook is realized
items.summary: The Bank of Japan (BoJ) board members shared their views on the monetary policy outlook on Wednesday, per the BoJ Minutes of the January meeting.

items.title: Fed’s Goolsbee: Energy shocks can pose risks to both sides of Fed mandate
items.summary: Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago President Austan Goolsbee said on Tuesday that energy shocks can pose risks to both sides of the central bank mandate.
